title: DRI skill+refs: deliver-to-merge lifecycle (open until merged; surface closed matches)
description:
Update the DRI skill and its references to the new deliver-to-merge lifecycle. Must be consistent with the ateam verbs delivered by the sibling bead (closed-match, reopen). DOCS ONLY — do not touch scripts/ateam or tests here.

SCOPE — these files ONLY:
- plugins/agent-teams/skills/dri/SKILL.md
- plugins/agent-teams/skills/dri/references/registry.md
- plugins/agent-teams/skills/dri/references/teardown.md

THE NEW LIFECYCLE (single source of truth for the edits):
- Delivery (PR opened) marks the initiative delivered + awaiting-merge via a status NOTE ("delivered: <PR URL> (awaiting merge)") and LEAVES THE INITIATIVE OPEN. Never close on PR-open.
- Close happens later, on a resume that confirms the PR is merged: close with reason "merged: <PR URL>".
- Local-only delivery (no origin remote -> no PR possible) is the ONE terminal-on-deliver case: close immediately with reason "delivered-local: <branch>@<short-sha>".
- TEAM teardown (shut down agents, remove worktrees, free ports) is DECOUPLED from INITIATIVE close. Teardown tears down the team but leaves the initiative OPEN whenever it is merely awaiting merge.

EDITS:

1) SKILL.md Phase 1 (register-or-resume branch, lines ~34-47):
   - resume-match -> OPEN match -> resume as today. ADD: if the matched open initiative's notes show delivered/awaiting-merge, check PR merge state (gh pr view ... --json state,mergedAt); if merged, close it with "merged: <PR URL>" and report done; if not yet merged, report "awaiting merge" and stand by / address review.
   - NO open match -> run "<ateam> closed-match \"$PWD\"". If it returns a CLOSED initiative id: GATE the human (gate protocol) — show id, title, close reason — and ask: reopen+resume the existing initiative, or start fresh? Reopen = "<ateam> reopen <id>" then continue from its recovered state; fresh = register a new initiative.
   - No match from either -> register (as today).
   - Replace the line "Closed initiatives never match." with the new closed-match behavior.

2) SKILL.md Phase 5 Deliver (line ~68): change "...then close the initiative issue with the PR as reason" to the new rule: record a "delivered" status note with the PR link, mark awaiting-merge, LEAVE OPEN; close only on confirmed merge (later resume). Local-only exception -> close "delivered-local: <branch>@<short-sha>".

3) SKILL.md Phase 6 Teardown (line ~70-72): state that teardown decouples TEAM teardown from INITIATIVE close — tear down the team but leave the initiative OPEN if awaiting merge.

4) references/registry.md:
   - Document the closed-match verb and reopen verb alongside resume-match (line ~22).
   - Change "Close on delivery: ... --reason 'delivered: <PR URL>'" (line ~27) to the new model: delivery records "delivered: <PR URL> (awaiting merge)" and leaves OPEN; close on merge with "merged: <PR URL>"; local-only terminal close "delivered-local: <branch>@<short-sha>". Document that awaiting-merge is tracked via a status note (no new label).

5) references/teardown.md step 8 (Registry, line ~12): change from "close the initiative with the PR link (if delivered)" to: final status note (delivered, PR link, awaiting-merge) and LEAVE OPEN — do NOT close; close happens on a later resume that confirms merge. Local-only (no PR): close with "delivered-local: <branch>@<short-sha>". Also clarify at the top that steps 1-7 tear down the TEAM regardless of initiative status.

Keep prose tight and match the existing terse doc voice. Do NOT push, do NOT merge.

title: ateam: add closed-match and reopen verbs (+ tests)
description:
Add two verbs to plugins/agent-teams/scripts/ateam and cover them in tests/ateam.test.sh. Keep resume-match, list, and list-json OPEN-only and unchanged (their output contracts are pinned by tests/ateam.test.sh:33-42 and relied on by hooks/scripts/compact-recovery.sh).

SCOPE — plugins/agent-teams/scripts/ateam, tests/ateam.test.sh ONLY.

VERB 1: closed-match <worktree-path>
- Mirrors resume-match exactly but queries --status=closed instead of --status=open.
- Prints the id of the CLOSED initiative whose description contains an exact line "worktree: <path>" (newest if several), or nothing on no match / error. Exit 0 either way (same robustness contract as resume-match).
- Use the same jq exact-line-match expression as resume-match (split on newline, any(. == $wt)); only the --status value differs.

VERB 2: reopen <id>
- Runs: bd -C "$ATH" reopen "<id>"  (verify bd has a reopen subcommand; beads ships one. If the exact flag differs, use the documented bd reopen form.)
- Missing id -> error to stderr, exit 2 (match the arg-guard style of the other verbs).

ALSO update, in the same file:
- The header Usage/Verbs comment block (lines ~7-9).
- BOTH the "" (empty verb) and "*" (unknown verb) usage printf blocks (lines ~224 and ~230) to list closed-match and reopen.

TESTS — append cases to tests/ateam.test.sh following the existing harness style (Case N comment header, FAIL message on mismatch):
- closed-match returns the id of a CLOSED initiative whose description has an exact "worktree: <path>" line.
- closed-match returns empty for a path that matches only an OPEN initiative (must NOT cross over).
- closed-match returns empty for a non-matching path and for a prefix path (prefix-collision guard, mirroring the resume-match cases).
- reopen flips a closed initiative back to open (assert via bd show / status afterwards), and resume-match then finds it while closed-match no longer does.
- Confirm the existing resume-match cases (3a/3b/3c) still pass unchanged.

Run tests/ateam.test.sh and paste the passing output in your completion report. Do NOT push, do NOT merge.
